titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Louis Daniel Brodsky Collection of William Heyen Papers
Brodsky (Louis Daniel) Collection of William Heyen Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 21, 2020 at 03:00

did.unittitle: Louis Daniel Brodsky collection of William Heyen papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 12-13 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Time Being Books on the Danford N. Barney, Jr. Fund, 2015.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: William Heyen (1940- ) is a poet, editor, literary critic, and educator, born in Brooklyn, New York. His poetry has been widely published including the collections The Swastika Poems (1977), Erika: Poems of the Holocaust (1984), Pterodactyl Rose (1991), Diana, Charles, & the Queen (1998), and Shoah Train (2003), among others. Falling from Heaven: Holocaust Poems of a Jew and a Gentile (1991) was co-written with Heyen's friend and fellow poet, Louis Daniel Brodsky. Over the years, Heyen received awards from the National Endowment for the Arts, the Guggenheim Foundation, and many more.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Louis Daniel Brodsky Collection of William Heyen Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, writings, printed material, proofs, production files, audio tapes, and other papers created by or relating to the life and work of William Heyen.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Louis Daniel Brodsky Collection of William Heyen Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts for poems including "The Dead," "The Secret," "The Power," and "Coin."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts for poems including "Mandorla" by Paul Celan and "Oval Medieval Painting From Which Christ Has Faded."

did.unittitle: Drafts--"A Professor of Literature" and "The Garden"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscript and typescript drafts for poems including "A Professor of Literature" and "The Garden."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poem by Paul Celan

did.unittitle: Drafts--A Voice From the Night at Belsen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts for poems including, "Unwilled Chaos," "Darkness,"Poem Touching the Gestapo," and "The Numinous."

did.unittitle: Drafts--Dark in the Reich of the Blond

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain drafts and publishing material for the anthology, "Erika," as well as a bound and annotated early typescript.

did.unittitle: Correspondence related to Cloud Hill Books
Postcard announcement related to "Dritte Reich des Traums"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This notebook contains manuscript drafts, notes, and writings from 1985. The notebook also includes a 1990 lay-in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain three bound manuscripts with mixed typed and manuscript material. The manuscripts are annotated, especially the first, which appears to give dates for when the poems were written initially.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts for poems including "The Rings" and the Epilogue of "Ribbons".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain 5 typescript drafts and 1 printed book mock-up (apparently by the publisher)-- not photocopy.Additionally, another folder contains draft material, a letter to Brodsky, and title page drafts (manuscript and typescript) within older folders (labeled "Water Turkeys" and "Elegy Composed on an Endless Drive Home").

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies of some notes, mostly between Brodsky and Heyen.

did.unittitle: Falling From Heaven book jacket mock-up

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains printed copies of book jacket versions, many of which are annotated or dated. Additionally, the text of a possible forword for "Falling From Heaven" is included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an undated photocopy of an early draft of "Falling," with manuscript annotations dated 1989.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Heyen's revision notes.

did.unittitle: Drafts--Copy Bill + I put Together in Weaving, Falling From Heaven

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a manuscript note, dated 1988, and a typescript.

did.unittitle: Annotated drafts--Chestnut Rain, "Who are these sleepers, soldiers, lovers" section

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts for poems including "The Psalm," "Science Fiction," and "That Socket," among other writings.

did.unittitle: Drafts--Chestnut Rain, "Darker / but light's blossom redolence" section
Drafts--Chestnut Rain, "I have spread out in front of me again,/ Leaves of a Tree"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts for poems including "From This Hour I Ordain Myself," "Prayer," and "Spruce in Winter."

did.unittitle: Drafts--Chestnut Rain, "Images and Shadows of Divine Things"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a bound draft of Chestnut Rain, though the title page indicateds "Snow Hen" was a working title at some point. The draft is almost entirely photocopy-- but seems to have been printed off sometime before 1984, given that the annotated edits are dated 1984.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts for poems including "The Circle," "Son Dream," and "Daughter Dream." Writings are mixed photocopy, manuscript and typescript.

did.unittitle: Drafts--Chestnut Rain, "The muse of this poem now relaxes her body in shadow"
Drafts--Chestnut Rain, "I am on a Flatcar" section
Drafts--Chestnut Rain, "My parents had not been born" section
Direct mail flyer samples for The Host

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains samples in color and original black and white.

did.unittitle: Preliminary bid requests for The Host
Interior text endorsements for The Host
Cover drafts and flap material for The Host
Drafts--"From the Night at Belsen" and "Swastika Poems"
Photocopied version--From the Night at Belsen
Sonnet and Haiku: Forms from the Reich University at Strasbourg
Shipping information for Clouds Hill Books
Correspondence related to Pterodactyl Rose reviews
Heyen's reading copy of Pterodactyl Rose
Heyen org. correspondence related to Pterodactyl Rose

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains typescript drafts of "The Garden" and "Professor of Literature."

did.unittitle: Original manuscript version of The Host

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains annotated typescript material and some photocopied material, also partially annotated.

did.unittitle: The Host with Jerry Call's corrections
The Host bound photocopy with insertions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a spiral-bound photocopy and inserted additions. The front cover has the inscription: "With inserted additions, SV's questions, BH's responses, LAL corrections."

did.unittitle: "Faulker's Forgotten People: The Indians of Yoknapatawpha" essay by Carl Withey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a version of the Blaine DeLancey Memorial Award Essay, written by Carl Withey.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Ribbons; The Gulf War, proof cassette

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
